data.content:
### POST /auth/v1/oauth/token

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/oauth-server/oauth-flows.mdx

Refreshes an expired access token using a refresh token. This endpoint implements the OAuth 2.0 refresh token grant type and returns a new access token and optionally a new refresh token.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/oauth/token

### Description
Refreshes an expired access token using a refresh token. Returns a new access token and may issue a new refresh token that should be stored to replace the old one.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token

### Request Body
- **grant_type** (string) - Required - Must be set to "refresh_token"
- **refresh_token** (string) - Required - The refresh token issued during authentication
- **client_id** (string) - Required - Your OAuth client ID

### Request Example
```
grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=v1.MXff...&client_id=<client-id>
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **access_token** (string) - New JWT access token
- **token_type** (string) - Token type, always "bearer"
- **expires_in** (integer) - Token expiration time in seconds
- **refresh_token** (string) - New refresh token (may be rotated)
- **scope** (string) - Space-separated list of granted scopes

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGc...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "v1.MXff...",
  "scope": "openid email profile"
}
```

### Notes
- Refresh tokens may be rotated; always update your stored refresh token when a new one is provided
- Set Content-Type header to "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
```

--------------------------------

### Refresh Access Token Function in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/oauth-server/oauth-flows.mdx

Async function that refreshes an expired access token using the Supabase OAuth token endpoint. Sends a POST request with the refresh token and client credentials to obtain a new access token. Throws an error if the refresh request fails.

```javascript
async function refreshAccessToken(refreshToken) {
  const response = await fetch(`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
    },
    body: new URLSearchParams({
      grant_type: 'refresh_token',
      refresh_token: refreshToken,
      client_id: '<client-id>',
    }),
  })

  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error('Failed to refresh token')
  }

  return await response.json()
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /auth/v1/oauth/token - Refresh Token Flow

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/oauth-server/oauth-flows.mdx

Endpoint for exchanging a refresh token to obtain a new access token and potentially a new refresh token, ensuring continuous user sessions without requiring re-authentication.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/oauth/token\n\n### Description\nThis endpoint allows clients to exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token, and optionally a new refresh token, without requiring the user to re-authenticate. This is essential for managing token expiration and maintaining continuous user sessions. Clients should initiate a refresh when the access token expires, is about to expire, or if an API call returns a 401 Unauthorized error.\n\n### Method\nPOST\n\n### Endpoint\n`https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token`\n\n### Parameters\n#### Request Body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)\n- **grant_type** (string) - Required - Specifies the type of grant being requested. Must be `refresh_token`.\n- **refresh_token** (string) - Required - The refresh token obtained during the initial authentication flow.\n- **client_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier for your application (client).\n- **client_secret** (string) - Optional - The client secret, required for confidential clients to authenticate.\n\n### Request Example\n```bash\ncurl -X POST 'https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token' \\\n  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \\\n  -d 'grant_type=refresh_token' \\\n  -d 'refresh_token=<your-refresh-token>' \\\n  -d 'client_id=<your-client-id>' \\\n  -d 'client_secret=<your-client-secret>'\n```\n\n### Response\n#### Success Response (200)\nUpon successful refresh, the API returns a JSON object containing the new tokens, similar to the initial token response.\n- **access_token** (string) - A new JWT access token.\n- **token_type** (string) - Always `bearer`.\n- **expires_in** (integer) - The lifetime of the new access token in seconds.\n- **refresh_token** (string) - A new refresh token (if refresh token rotation is enabled).\n- **scope** (string) - The granted scopes.\n- **id_token** (string) - An OpenID Connect ID token, if `openid` scope was requested.\n\n#### Response Example\n```json\n{\n  "access_token": "eyJhbGc...",\n  "token_type": "bearer",\n  "expires_in": 3600,\n  "refresh_token": "MXff...",\n  "scope": "openid email profile",\n  "id_token": "eyJhbGc..."\n}\n```
```

--------------------------------

### Sign In with OAuth and Extract Google Provider Tokens

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/social-login/auth-google.mdx

Initiates OAuth sign-in with Google using Supabase and passes parameters to enable refresh token extraction. The `access_type: 'offline'` and `prompt: 'consent'` parameters instruct Google to return a refresh token. The provider_token and provider_refresh_token are available in the returned session data.

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
const supabase = createClient('https://your-project.supabase.co', 'sb_publishable_... or anon key')

const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
  provider: 'google',
  options: {
    queryParams: {
      access_type: 'offline',
      prompt: 'consent',
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Sign in with Azure to obtain provider refresh token (offline_access scope)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/social-login/auth-azure.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to request the 'offline_access' scope during Azure OAuth sign-in. This is necessary if you need the 'provider_refresh_token' from Azure, as it is not returned by default.

```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
const supabase = createClient('https://your-project.supabase.co', 'sb_publishable_... or anon key')

// ---cut---
async function signInWithAzure() {
  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
    provider: 'azure',
    options: {
      scopes: 'offline_access',
    },
  })
}
```

```dart
Future<void> signInWithAzure() async {
  await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth(
    OAuthProvider.azure,
    scopes: 'offline_access',
  );
}
```

```kotlin
suspend fun signInWithAzure() {
    supabase.auth.signInWith(Azure) {
        scopes.add("offline_access")
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Supabase OAuth Refresh Token Request (Public Client)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/oauth-server/oauth-flows.mdx

This cURL command demonstrates how to initiate a refresh token flow for a public client. It sends a POST request to the Supabase OAuth token endpoint, providing "grant_type", "refresh_token", and "client_id" in the request body to obtain a new access token.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  -d 'grant_type=refresh_token' \
  -d 'refresh_token=<refresh-token>' \
  -d 'client_id=<client-id>'
```

--------------------------------

### Set Up Authentication Token Refresh Proxy in Next.js

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/server-side/creating-a-client.mdx

Implements middleware to automatically refresh expired Supabase Auth tokens and update cookies in both the server and browser. The proxy validates JWT signatures using getClaims() instead of getSession() for security. Includes a matcher to optimize performance by skipping routes that don't access Supabase.

```typescript
import { type NextRequest } from 'next/server'
import { updateSession } from '@/lib/supabase/proxy'

export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  return await updateSession(request)
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    /*
     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
     * - _next/static (static files)
     * - _next/image (image optimization files)
     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
     */
    '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /auth/v1/token - Token Refresh Rate Limit

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/_partials/auth_rate_limits.mdx

Rate limiting for token refresh requests, limited by IP address with burst allowances.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/token - Token Refresh Requests

### Description
Rate limiting for token refresh requests to obtain new access tokens.

### Endpoint
`POST /auth/v1/token`

### Rate Limit
- **Limited By**: IP Address
- **Requests Per Hour**: Configurable via `auth.rate_limits.token_refresh.requests_per_hour`
- **Burst Allowance**: Up to `auth.rate_limits.token_refresh.requests_burst` requests
- **Customization**: Is customizable

### Request Body
- **grant_type** (string) - Required - Must be "refresh_token"
- **refresh_token** (string) - Required - Valid refresh token

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **access_token** (string) - New access token
- **token_type** (string) - Token type (Bearer)
- **expires_in** (integer) - Token expiration time in seconds
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/oauth/token (Refresh Token)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/integrations/build-a-supabase-oauth-integration.mdx

Uses a refresh token to obtain a new access token when the current one expires. This ensures continuous access to user data without requiring re-authentication.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/oauth/token

### Description
Refreshes an expired access token using a previously obtained refresh token.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
https://api.supabase.com/v1/oauth/token

### Parameters
#### Request Body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
- **grant_type** (string) - Required - Must be `refresh_token`.
- **refresh_token** (string) - Required - The refresh token obtained during the initial authorization code exchange.

#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Basic authentication header in the format `Basic <base64_encoded(client_id:client_secret)>`.
  - **client_id** (string) - The unique client ID identifying your OAuth App.
  - **client_secret** (string) - The secret that authenticates your OAuth App to Supabase.

### Request Example
```json
// Example application/x-www-form-urlencoded body
grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=YOUR_REFRESH_TOKEN
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **access_token** (string) - The new access token.
- **expires_in** (integer) - The lifetime in seconds of the new access token.
- **refresh_token** (string) - Optional - A new refresh token, if the server issues rotating refresh tokens.
- **token_type** (string) - The type of token issued (e.g., "Bearer").

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.new_access_token",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "new_refresh_token_string",
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### access_token - Authentication Token Update

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/realtime/protocol.mdx

Message used to setup a new authentication token or refresh an existing token for Realtime. Prevents private channels from closing when tokens expire and enables token rotation for security.

```APIDOC
## access_token - Authentication Token Update

### Description
Message used to setup a new authentication token or refresh an existing token for Realtime authentication. This prevents private channels from closing when tokens expire.

### Message Type
access_token

### Payload Structure
```json
{
  "access_token": string
}
```

### Parameters
- **access_token** (string) - Required - The new or refreshed JWT access token for authentication

### Use Cases
- Setup new token for authentication
- Refresh token to prevent private channel closure on expiration
- Rotate tokens for security purposes

### Protocol Version 2.0.0 Example
```json
[
  "10",
  "1",
  "realtime:chat-room",
  "access_token",
  {
    "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiYWRtaW4iOnRydWUsImlhdCI6MTUxNjIzOTAyMn0.KMUFsIDTnFmyG3nMiGM6H9FNFUROf3wh7SmqJp-QV30"
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### Refresh Realtime Authentication Token in Supabase Clients

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/realtime/postgres-changes.mdx

This code demonstrates how to update the authentication token for your Realtime connection using different Supabase client libraries. After generating a new token, pass it to the Realtime client to ensure continued authenticated access. Note that Kotlin handles token updates at the channel level.

```js
supabase.realtime.setAuth('fresh-token')
```

```dart
supabase.realtime.setAuth('fresh-token');
```

```swift
await supabase.realtime.setAuth("fresh-token")
```

```kotlin
myChannel.updateAuth("fresh-token")
```

```python
supabase.realtime.set_auth('fresh-token')
```

--------------------------------

### Flutter: Sign in with Apple using ID Token

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/social-login/auth-apple.mdx

Implements Sign in with Apple for Flutter apps on iOS and macOS using the `sign_in_with_apple` package. It generates a nonce, retrieves the ID token from Apple, and then signs the user in with Supabase.

```dart
import 'package:sign_in_with_apple/sign_in_with_apple.dart';
import 'package:supabase_flutter/supabase_flutter.dart';
import 'package:crypto/crypto.dart';

/// Performs Apple sign in on iOS or macOS
Future<AuthResponse> signInWithApple() async {
  final rawNonce = supabase.auth.generateRawNonce();
  final hashedNonce = sha256.convert(utf8.encode(rawNonce)).toString();

  final credential = await SignInWithApple.getAppleIDCredential(
    scopes: [
      AppleIDAuthorizationScopes.email,
      AppleIDAuthorizationScopes.fullName,
    ],
    nonce: hashedNonce,
  );

  final idToken = credential.identityToken;
  if (idToken == null) {
    throw const AuthException(
        'Could not find ID Token from generated credential.');
  }

  final authResponse = await supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken(
    provider: OAuthProvider.apple,
    idToken: idToken,
    nonce: rawNonce,
  );

  // Apple only provides the user's full name on the first sign-in
  // Save it to user metadata if available
  if (credential.givenName != null || credential.familyName != null) {
    final nameParts = <String>[];
    if (credential.givenName != null) nameParts.add(credential.givenName!);
    if (credential.familyName != null) nameParts.add(credential.familyName!);

    final fullName = nameParts.join(' ');

```

--------------------------------

### Perform Supabase OAuth Refresh Token Request (Confidential Client)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/oauth-server/oauth-flows.mdx

This cURL command illustrates a refresh token request for confidential clients, which additionally requires the "client_secret". The POST request targets the Supabase OAuth token endpoint, including "grant_type", "refresh_token", "client_id", and "client_secret" in the form data.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/oauth/token' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  -d 'grant_type=refresh_token' \
  -d 'refresh_token=<refresh-token>' \
  -d 'client_id=<client-id>' \
  -d 'client_secret=<client-secret>'
```

--------------------------------

### POST signInWithOAuth - Google OAuth Flow

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/social-login/auth-google.mdx

Initiates a Google OAuth 2.0 sign-in flow and retrieves provider tokens. Include access_type and prompt parameters to obtain refresh tokens from Google.

```APIDOC
## POST signInWithOAuth

### Description
Initiates OAuth sign-in with Google provider. Extracts provider tokens for accessing Google services on behalf of the user. Requires specific query parameters to obtain refresh tokens.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth()

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **provider** (string) - Required - OAuth provider identifier, must be 'google'
- **options.queryParams.access_type** (string) - Required - Set to 'offline' to receive refresh token
- **options.queryParams.prompt** (string) - Required - Set to 'consent' to force consent screen

### Request Example
```js
const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
  provider: 'google',
  options: {
    queryParams: {
      access_type: 'offline',
      prompt: 'consent'
    }
  }
})
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **session.provider_token** (string) - Google OAuth 2.0 access token
- **session.provider_refresh_token** (string) - Google refresh token (if offline access requested)
- **user** (object) - Authenticated user object

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "data": {
    "session": {
      "provider_token": "ya29.a0AfH6SMBx...",
      "provider_refresh_token": "1//0gF..."
    },
    "user": {
      "id": "user-uuid",
      "email": "user@example.com"
    }
  },
  "error": null
}
```
```
